> Aegis: Now heals 1hp per turn.

okay?
> Gauss Charge: Now comes with a second buildtime 3 Gauss Charge
perhaps too pushed
> Centrifuge: Now costs 6, has a buildtime of 5, and produces 19GGBBRR

> Ebb Turbine: Now costs 3B and produces 1 less gold per turn, health is 3 instead of 4
i supported therefore its good

--- Thread (1 messages, 2020-12-10) ---
[2020-12-10 16:54] randomflyingtaco (RiFT (#RIPBrowserers)): Swords to Plowshares (2 :Blue: , 1 Supply) 3 :Health: 
:Frontline: 
At the start of your turn, remove all plow counters, gain 1 :Gold::Energy:and a plow counter for each :Attack: you have, and lose all :Attack:.
:Click: , pay 1 :Gold::Energy: for each plow counter you have, and sacrifice Swords to Plowshares: gain :Attack: for each plow counter you have

Was thinking about what a temporary ceasefire unit looks like that doesn't break SDR and doesn't have ordering exploits, and I concluded that it would need to have some new counter system.  Added frontline as a way that the other player could provoke you into fighting back, but you do get at least 1 guaranteed turn, so I guess you could use it to exploit a bunch of :Lifespan: units. Thought about it being a spell that gives the unit to both players, like a truce (and maybe the click would destroy both copies like breaking a truce), but it was already complicated enough. Yes, I know the ability only works on start of turn attackers, but if the :Attack: -> :Gold: :Energy: conversion was on a click then there would be a bunch of ordering inconsistencies. Also, I did it as click sac, because the alternative of click every turn to extend lifespan would also need another new mechanic (gain 1 :Exhaust: wouldn't work, right?).
tl;dr Is it an interesting unit with justifiable complexity that fits within Prismata? Not really, I just wanted to think about how the card would be written.
